similar_questions.title: The Score of Students Solving Math Expression

discussion_posts.name: chymcakmilan
discussion_posts.content: If first item is not number or bracket, just add 0 ..it makes it much easier.\n `"-2+ 1"` becomes `0 - 2 + 1`

discussion_posts.name: lkwq007
discussion_posts.content:
Input:
`"-2+ 1"`
`"- (3 + (4 + 5))"`

However, the problem clearly states that
> '-' could be used as a unary operation but it has to be inside parentheses

discussion_posts.name: TERNION2205
discussion_posts.content:
Sat for the whole day to figure out its solution,
Got it using simple recursion and a bunch of conditionals.
Just to figure out it was NOT enough to pass tests 43 and onwards.
Confused whether I should be glad to find out the "at least working" solution, or should sit for another day to get this problem done :c

discussion_posts.name: TheAsh
discussion_posts.content: I think that the problem description should include information on the desired efficiency. I\'m probably not the only one who first designed a working solution that got rejected after test `36` because of its runtime, and then had to go back and write a solution in `O(N)`. I find it frustrating when information that can be easily added to the problem statement has to be inferred from test cases. Regarding this problem, jus adding something along the lines of "just make a single pass on the input string" would be fine.
